help: Dirichlet-distributed shares over CAV automation levels and STI infrastructure levels. These are scenario-defining assumptions about how the fleet distributes across capability tiers — they are not residual empirical uncertainty. The Dirichlet concentration controls the spread around the assumed mix; it lacks an external empirical anchor and is a modeling design choice.

parameters.label: CAV level mix (L3 / L4 / L5)
parameters.physical_meaning: Simplex over the three CAV automation levels. This is a scenario-defining assumption about how the autonomous fleet distributes across L3 (conditional automation), L4 (high automation), and L5 (full automation) capability tiers. The Dirichlet concentration is a modeling choice: higher alpha = more concentrated around the assumed mean; lower alpha = wider spread across the simplex.
parameters.citation: No external empirical anchor for the concentration. The Dirichlet alpha is a modeling assumption; SAE J3016 defines the automation levels.
parameters.why_default_free: Level mix is a scenario assumption. LOW triples alpha for a tighter simplex while preserving the mean; MEDIUM retains the original wider spread. The spread itself is a modeling choice, not measured empirical uncertainty.
parameters.why_high_rejected: Loosening alpha below MEDIUM produces near-uniform simplex draws that have no physical basis.

parameters.label: STI level mix (Basic / Semi / Highly)
parameters.physical_meaning: Simplex over the three STI infrastructure levels. This is a scenario-defining assumption about the distribution of smart-traffic infrastructure across capability tiers (Basic roadside sensing, Semi-automated signal coordination, Highly-automated corridor management).
parameters.citation: No external empirical anchor for the concentration. Modeling assumption.
parameters.why_default_free: Same rationale as F18. Scenario assumption, not empirical uncertainty.
